vision.text: A tiny tool that counts how many distinct authors appear in a JSON file of receipts.

spec.text: Build a single-file Python tool, run as `python3 tool.py <receipts_json_path>`. Read a JSON shaped {"receipts": [{"author": "..."}, ...]}. Print exactly one line: 'AUTHORS: <N>' where N is the count of distinct (case-sensitive, exact) author strings. On invalid JSON, print 'error:' and exit 0. Standard library only.
spec.prompt: What does the team want made real?

source_offerings.title: Hand-rolled receipt fixture
source_offerings.cleared_text: A small JSON file with 5 fictional receipts across 3 authors.

witnessed_working.description: Tuesday: read a small JSON fixture, see the AUTHORS line, recognize it counts what was expected.
acceptance_criteria: prints a line starting with 'error:' on invalid input, exits 0
